judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent substance and proposed fixes; they are comparable in coverage, with Report 2 adding minor extra context (e.g. compliance.md, prompt files) without changing the core findings.

report_1.evidence: `call_with_fallback` catches all of them identically via the single bare `except Exception as e:` — it logs a warning (`log_fallover`) and unconditionally resends the same `payload` to `secondary.complete(payload)`. There is no differentiation by error type at all. ... Fix: replace the catch-all with an explicit non-retryable denylist that re-raises instead of failing over: NON_RETRYABLE = (BadRequestError, ContentPolicyError, AuthError)

report_2.evidence: `call_with_fallback` catches all of them (and everything else) identically via bare `except Exception`, logs a warning via `log_fallover` ... and unconditionally retries the same `payload` against `secondary.complete`. ... Fix: catch the specific transient classes (`RateLimitError`, `RequestTimeoutError`, `ServerError`) and fall over to secondary only for those; handle `BadRequestError` and `ContentPolicyError` as their own non-retried cases (surface/quarantine rather than resend); treat `AuthError` as fail-fast/alertable rather than a per-call fallover

report_1.evidence: if `os.environ.get("STAGE")` is anything other than the literal string "production" ... returns True unconditionally, before ever touching redis. ... returns True, i.e. fails open. ... Fix: ... invert both fail-open branches to fail closed, replace the env-name check with a narrow explicit dev-only override

report_2.evidence: returns True unconditionally, for any value other than the exact string "production" ... Path C ... redis.incr raises RedisError → returns True (fail-open). ... Fix: use an explicit allowlist/inversion for non-prod detection instead of != "production" (fail closed on ambiguity) ... and fail closed (return False, ...) on RedisError rather than unconditionally allowing all traffic.

report_1.evidence: record.input_text — the full intake document text ... embedded whole in the log line. record.metadata — an arbitrary, unbounded dict passed straight through with no filtering. There is no allowlist of safe fields at all ... Fix: log only an explicit allowlist, use the already-computed pseudonymized id, and hash/truncate free text

report_2.evidence: This writes the raw, non-pseudonymized applicant_id and the full raw input_text ... plus an arbitrary, unvalidated metadata dict ... Fix: never log input_text (or replace with a redacted excerpt/length/hash if traceability is needed) ... and whitelist specific non-sensitive fields from metadata rather than logging it wholesale.

report_1.evidence: if PII_SALT is unset ... the .get(..., "") default silently substitutes an empty string. There is no exception, no warning, no log line — the function degrades silently to sha256(applicant_id) ... Fix: fail hard on a missing salt rather than defaulting: ... raise RuntimeError("PII_SALT is not configured; refusing to pseudonymize with an unsalted hash")

report_2.evidence: When PII_SALT is unset, os.environ.get("PII_SALT", "") silently defaults to the empty string — the function does not raise, warn, or log anything; it just returns SHA256(applicant_id). ... Fix: fail fast if PII_SALT is missing (os.environ["PII_SALT"], raising KeyError, or an explicit startup-time config check) rather than silently defaulting to ""
